title: Podman 2.0 fails to create container with hostname complaining about host UTS namespace
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Since the upgrade to 2.0.0.rc7 on CentOS 8 Stream, containers can't be created anymore which used to be created.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. "podman", "container", "run", "--name", "nginx-cnt", "--hostname", "nginx-host", "--pod", "ncpod", "--volume", "nextcloud-vol:/var/www/html:z,ro", "--volume", "/var/lib/containers/sources/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:Z,ro", "--env", "VIRTUAL_HOST=fantasio.lavarde.eu", "--env", "LETSENCRYPT_HOST=fantasio.lavarde.eu", "--env", "LETSENCRYPT_EMAIL=le@mymail.com", "--network", "proxy-nw,podman", "--detach", "nginx:stable-alpine"

**Describe the results you received:**

return code 125 and error message: "Error: invalid config provided: cannot set hostname when running in the host UTS namespace: invalid configuration"

**Describe the results you expected:**

With 1.6.4 the container would be created.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The issue always happen, already for 2 containers (I don't dare destroy more).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.0.0-rc7
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64

  os: linux

    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:

  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.0-0.9.rc7.module_el8.3.0+432+2e9cbcd8.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical server, entirely setup with kickstart and ansible, available from https://gitlab.com/EricPublic/miscericlaneous/-/blob/master/automate/redfish_ipmi/misc/ks_centos8stream.cfg and https://gitlab.com/EricPublic/miscericlaneous/-/blob/ericzolf-own-nginx-proxy/nextcloud_container/setup_nextcloud_container.yml

comments.body: The error message here is clearly not correct, but the fact that it is giving an error is correct. Using `--hostname` with a pod that shares the UTS namespace (as it does by default) is not supported, and the fact that it worked before is a bug. You can set the hostname for the entire pod, but individual containers inside a pod must use the pod's hostname.
I think this should be left open to fix the error message, though, that's rather egregious.

@ericzolf If you still want to use different hostnames for your containers inside the pod you can create the pod like this: `podman pod create --name test --share net,cgroup,ipc`. With that option the UTS namespace will not get shared in the pod and thus you can set a hostname for the container.

You should be able to force a private UTS namespace for just one container with `--uts=private` that would allow you to set a hostname for a container within a pod (at the cost of losing a shared `/dev/shm` and other shared IPC constructs).

Thanks to everybody, now I have many options and can't decide which one to use.

Just joking but it's truly difficult to understand what the actual impact is of the choices. @mheon isn't IPC another namespace than UTS, if I refer to https://lwn.net/Articles/531114/ ? Does the hostname only impact what hostname, uname & Co returns, or also the inter-container dynamic DNS managed by podman (that was at least the case with docker IIRC)?

And, yes, I agree that the error message could be improved. From what I understood so far, I would suggest something like: `the hostname of a container can't be set when sharing the UTS namespace with a pod. Check options --uts for the container or --share for the pod creation.`

Looking through the man pages, I also see that the description for `--uts` isn't correct:

1. `private` is obviously _not_ the default when the container is created within a pod (is it then `host`, the "host" being the pod? Is it also insecure somehow?)
2. for `ns` it's not clear what an "own" namespace makes a difference to a private one.
3. a note that the uts mode `host` (and/or whichever mode is used within a pod) keeps one from using the --hostname option could be helpful.

1. you will join the pods UTS namespace so it will be a private namespace which is shared between all containers inside the pod.
2. ns expects a path to an existing UTS namespace which the container then joins

I opened #7352 to update the docs and error message.
